file.content:
name: Jira Ticket agent

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '17 7 * * *'
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  groom:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - name: Install jira CLI
        run: bash scripts/install-jira-cli.sh

      - name: Put bin/ on PATH
        run: echo "$PWD/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: Write jira config
        env:
          JIRA_SERVER: ${{ vars.JIRA_SERVER }}
          JIRA_LOGIN: ${{ vars.JIRA_LOGIN }}
          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
          JIRA_BOARD_ID: ${{ vars.JIRA_BOARD_ID }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          : "${JIRA_SERVER:?JIRA_SERVER repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_LOGIN:?JIRA_LOGIN repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY:?JIRA_PROJECT_KEY repo variable is required}"
          mkdir -p .jira
          cat > .jira/config.yml <<EOF
          installation: Cloud
          server: ${JIRA_SERVER}
          login: ${JIRA_LOGIN}
          project:
              key: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}
              type: classic
          board:
              id: ${JIRA_BOARD_ID:-0}
          EOF

      - id: prompt
        env:
          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}"
            echo
            cat agents/jira-ticket/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        env:
          JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
          JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          claude_env: |
            JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
            JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
            JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
          claude_args: |
            --allowedTools "Bash(jira:*),Bash(bin/jira:*),Read,Glob,Grep,Write"

      - name: Post Slack summary
        if: always() && hashFiles('slack-summary.md') != ''
        env:
          SLACK_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SLACK_MCP_XOXB_TOKEN }}
          CHANNEL_ID: ${{ vars.SLACK_OBSERVABILITY_CHANNEL_ID }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          : "${SLACK_TOKEN:?SLACK_MCP_XOXB_TOKEN secret is required}"
          : "${CHANNEL_ID:?SLACK_OBSERVABILITY_CHANNEL_ID repo variable is required}"
          payload=$(jq -n \
            --arg channel "${CHANNEL_ID}" \
            --rawfile text slack-summary.md \
            '{channel: $channel, text: $text, mrkdwn: true}')
          resp=$(curl -sS -X POST https://slack.com/api/chat.postMessage \
            -H "Authorization: Bearer ${SLACK_TOKEN}" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8" \
            --data "${payload}")
          echo "${resp}" | jq .
          [ "$(echo "${resp}" | jq -r .ok)" = "true" ]

      - name: Commit execution log to main
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
          git checkout -B main origin/main
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/jira-ticket/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp "$LOG_PATH" "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: jira-ticket run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin main

      - name: Trigger prompt improver
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
        run: |
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          gh workflow run prompt-improver.yml \
            -f agent_name=jira-ticket \
            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/jira-ticket/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
            -f log_ref=main

content.content:
1	name: PR Review agent
2	
3	on:
4	  pull_request:
5	    types: [opened, synchronize, ready_for_review, reopened]
6	    paths-ignore:
7	      - '.github/agent-logs/**'
8	      - 'agents/**/prompt.md'
9	  workflow_dispatch:
10	
11	jobs:
12	  review:
13	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
14	    permissions:
15	      contents: write
16	      pull-requests: write
17	      id-token: write
18	      actions: write
19	    steps:
20	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
21	        with:
22	          fetch-depth: 1
23	
24	      - id: prompt
25	        run: |
26	          {
27	            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
28	            echo "REPO: ${{ github.repository }}"
29	            echo "PR NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
30	            echo
31	            cat agents/pr-review/prompt.md
32	            echo PROMPT_EOF
33	          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
34	
35	      - id: claude
36	        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
37	        with:
38	          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
39	          track_progress: false
40	          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
41	          claude_args: |
42	            --allowedTools "mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment,Bash(gh pr comment:*),Bash(gh pr diff:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Read,Grep,Glob"
43	
44	      - name: Commit execution log to PR branch
45	        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
46	        env:
47	          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
48	          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
49	          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
50	          BRANCH: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
51	        run: |
52	          set -e
53	          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
54	          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
55	          git fetch --depth=1 origin "$BRANCH"
56	          git checkout -B "$BRANCH" "origin/$BRANCH"
57	          DEST=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json"
58	          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
59	          cp "$LOG_PATH" "$DEST"
60	          git add "$DEST"
61	          git commit -m "agent log: PR #${PR_NUMBER} run ${RUN_ID}"
62	          git push origin "$BRANCH"
63	
64	      - name: Trigger prompt improver
65	        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
66	        env:
67	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
68	          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
69	          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
70	          LOG_REF: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
71	        run: |
72	          gh workflow run prompt-improver.yml \
73	            -f agent_name=pr-review \
74	            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
75	            -f log_ref="${LOG_REF}"
76

file.content:
name: PR Review agent

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, ready_for_review, reopened]
    paths-ignore:
      - '.github/agent-logs/**'
      - 'agents/**/prompt.md'
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  review:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - id: prompt
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "REPO: ${{ github.repository }}"
            echo "PR NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
            echo
            cat agents/pr-review/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          track_progress: false
          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          claude_args: |
            --allowedTools "mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment,Bash(gh pr comment:*),Bash(gh pr diff:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Read,Grep,Glob"

      - name: Commit execution log to PR branch
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
          BRANCH: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git fetch --depth=1 origin "$BRANCH"
          git checkout -B "$BRANCH" "origin/$BRANCH"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp "$LOG_PATH" "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: PR #${PR_NUMBER} run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin "$BRANCH"

      - name: Trigger prompt improver
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_REF: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
        run: |
          gh workflow run prompt-improver.yml \
            -f agent_name=pr-review \
            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/pr-review/pr-${PR_NUMBER}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
            -f log_ref="${LOG_REF}"

content.thinking:
Now let me check some more things:

1. The KAN-1 description references the log-commit step at `.github/workflows/jira-ticket.yml:97-114`. Let me verify: 
   - Line 97: `- name: Commit execution log to main` ✓
   - Line 114: `git push origin main` ✓
   - So the reference in KAN-1 is correct.

2. KAN-2 has a broken path in AC: "Run log committed under .github/agent-logs/junior-dev//.json" - the `DATE` part is missing, it should probably be `.github/agent-logs/junior-dev/<DATE>/<RUN_ID>.json`. 

3. KAN-5 references `docs/agentic-workflow.md` section "Design choices" and also `.github/workflows/pr-review.yml:57`. Looking at line 57 of pr-review.yml, that's the DEST variable assignment rather than the step header itself—the actual log-commit step starts earlier at line 44, so the line reference is slightly off.

4. KAN-5 correctly notes that `pr-review` is event-triggered on pull_request events, not cron-scheduled, which matches the actual workflow configuration.

5. KAN-5 has no blocking dependency on an existing ticket since `pr-review` is already a workflow rather than a Jira ticket—it can operate independently once the `agents/pr-review/` directory exists.

6. KAN-2's description mentions `/goal` or `/ralph-loop` commands referenced in `docs/agentic-workflow.md`, so I need to verify those actually exist in the documentation.

content.content:
1	# Agentic Development Workflow
2	
3	> **Status:** First-draft vision. Extracted from a hand-drawn diagram (2026-06-04).
4	> Not yet scoped, designed, or implemented.
5	
6	The flow describes an end-to-end pipeline where humans create tickets and merge
7	final PRs, and a chain of agents handles everything in between: ticket grooming,
8	complexity routing, implementation, testing, review, and refinement.
9	
10	## High-level flow
11	
12	```
13	Humans create tickets
14	        │
15	        ▼
16	   Ticket Agent
17	        │
18	        ▼
19	Development Orchestrator
20	        │
21	        ├──── complex ────► Senior developer agent  (/openspec:explore)
22	        │                          │
23	        │                          ▼
24	        │                    PR Review agent ◄────┐
25	        │                          ▲              │
26	        │                          ▼              │
27	        │                    PR Refinement agent  │
28	        │                          │              │
29	        │                          ▼              │
30	        │                Humans test & merge PR   │
31	        │                                         │
32	        └──── easy ──────► Junior developer agent │
33	                                  │   (/goal or /ralph-loop)
34	                                  ▼               │
35	                            Testing agent ────────┘
36	                          (loops back to dev
37	                           agent on failure)
38	```
39	
40	## Human touchpoints
41	
42	- **Start:** humans create tickets.
43	- **End:** humans test the final PR and merge it.
44	- **Mid-loop (complex path only):** the Senior developer agent pulls a human
45	  into the loop before implementing.
46	
47	## Agents
48	
49	### Ticket Agent
50	**Goal:** manage Jira tickets.
51	
52	- Does **not** invent new tickets.
53	- Works on a daily basis. 
54	- Decomposes tickets and improves descriptions based on the current code.
55	- Creates dependencies between tickets (`blocks`, `depends on`).
56	
57	### Development Orchestrator
58	**Goal:** decide which development agent to launch for each ticket.
59	
60	- Reads the ticket.
61	- Decides the complexity (complex vs. easy) and routes accordingly.
62	
63	### Senior Developer Agent — `/openspec:explore`
64	**Goal:** tackle complex tasks **with a human in the loop**.
65	
66	- Uses spec-driven development.
67	- Requests human input before moving to implementation.
68	
69	### Junior Developer Agent — `/goal` or `/ralph-loop`
70	**Goal:** tackle easy tasks **without a human in the loop**.
71	
72	- Uses headless Claude, planning straight through to execution.
73	- Creates a PR and launches the testing agent.
74	
75	### Testing Agent
76	**Goal:** write dedicated tests and run them.
77	
78	- If tests fail, hands the work back to the developer agent.
79	
80	### PR Review Agent
81	**Goal:** daily review of updated PRs.
82	
83	- Inline comments.
84	- Severity.
85	- Reasoning.
86	
87	### PR Refinement Agent
88	**Goal:** daily refinement of the PR.
89	
90	- May use a different model (e.g. CODEX).
91	- Counteracts the review (acts as a deliberate counter-voice to the reviewer).
92	
93	## Design choices worth calling out
94	
95	- **Complexity gate at the orchestrator.** Two-tier routing keeps the
96	  human-in-the-loop cost only on work that needs it.
97	- **Junior ↔ Testing tight loop.** The easy path stays fully autonomous —
98	  failures bounce inside the loop, not back to a human.
99	- **Review ↔ Refinement as an adversarial pair.** Using a different model for
100	  refinement is a deliberate hedge against single-model review bias.
101	- **Daily cadence** on review and refinement rather than per-PR-event triggers.
102	
103	## Open questions (for follow-up)
104	
105	- How is "complex vs. easy" actually decided? Heuristic, classifier, or LLM
106	  judgment with calibration?
107	- Where does the orchestrator live (cron, webhook, queue)?
108	- What stops the Junior↔Testing loop from running forever on a hard ticket?
109	- How does the Refinement agent know when to stop counteracting?
110	- How are the two daily passes (review + refinement) sequenced — same run, or
111	  staggered?
112

file.content:
# Agentic Development Workflow

> **Status:** First-draft vision. Extracted from a hand-drawn diagram (2026-06-04).
> Not yet scoped, designed, or implemented.

The flow describes an end-to-end pipeline where humans create tickets and merge
final PRs, and a chain of agents handles everything in between: ticket grooming,
complexity routing, implementation, testing, review, and refinement.

## High-level flow

```
Humans create tickets
        │
        ▼
   Ticket Agent
        │
        ▼
Development Orchestrator
        │
        ├──── complex ────► Senior developer agent  (/openspec:explore)
        │                          │
        │                          ▼
        │                    PR Review agent ◄────┐
        │                          ▲              │
        │                          ▼              │
        │                    PR Refinement agent  │
        │                          │              │
        │                          ▼              │
        │                Humans test & merge PR   │
        │                                         │
        └──── easy ──────► Junior developer agent │
                                  │   (/goal or /ralph-loop)
                                  ▼               │
                            Testing agent ────────┘
                          (loops back to dev
                           agent on failure)
```

## Human touchpoints

- **Start:** humans create tickets.
- **End:** humans test the final PR and merge it.
- **Mid-loop (complex path only):** the Senior developer agent pulls a human
  into the loop before implementing.

## Agents

### Ticket Agent
**Goal:** manage Jira tickets.

- Does **not** invent new tickets.
- Works on a daily basis. 
- Decomposes tickets and improves descriptions based on the current code.
- Creates dependencies between tickets (`blocks`, `depends on`).

### Development Orchestrator
**Goal:** decide which development agent to launch for each ticket.

- Reads the ticket.
- Decides the complexity (complex vs. easy) and routes accordingly.

### Senior Developer Agent — `/openspec:explore`
**Goal:** tackle complex tasks **with a human in the loop**.

- Uses spec-driven development.
- Requests human input before moving to implementation.

### Junior Developer Agent — `/goal` or `/ralph-loop`
**Goal:** tackle easy tasks **without a human in the loop**.

- Uses headless Claude, planning straight through to execution.
- Creates a PR and launches the testing agent.

### Testing Agent
**Goal:** write dedicated tests and run them.

- If tests fail, hands the work back to the developer agent.

### PR Review Agent
**Goal:** daily review of updated PRs.

- Inline comments.
- Severity.
- Reasoning.

### PR Refinement Agent
**Goal:** daily refinement of the PR.

- May use a different model (e.g. CODEX).
- Counteracts the review (acts as a deliberate counter-voice to the reviewer).

## Design choices worth calling out

- **Complexity gate at the orchestrator.** Two-tier routing keeps the
  human-in-the-loop cost only on work that needs it.
- **Junior ↔ Testing tight loop.** The easy path stays fully autonomous —
  failures bounce inside the loop, not back to a human.
- **Review ↔ Refinement as an adversarial pair.** Using a different model for
  refinement is a deliberate hedge against single-model review bias.
- **Daily cadence** on review and refinement rather than per-PR-event triggers.

## Open questions (for follow-up)

- How is "complex vs. easy" actually decided? Heuristic, classifier, or LLM
  judgment with calibration?
- Where does the orchestrator live (cron, webhook, queue)?
- What stops the Junior↔Testing loop from running forever on a hard ticket?
- How does the Refinement agent know when to stop counteracting?
- How are the two daily passes (review + refinement) sequenced — same run, or
  staggered?

content.thinking:
Now I have a full picture. Let me analyze each ticket against the rubric:

**KAN-1** - "Build Development Orchestrator agent (complexity gate)" - Status: In Progress
- Has Goal, Deliverables, Triggers, Allowed tools, Acceptance criteria
- The description references `.github/workflows/jira-ticket.yml:97-114` for the log-commit-to-main step
- Verified: Line 97 = `- name: Commit execution log to main`, Line 114 = `git push origin main` ✓
- AC: "A ticket judged complex dispatches senior-dev.yml; a ticket judged easy dispatches junior-dev.yml." - testable ✓
- The acceptance criteria about run logs being committed to `.github/agent-logs/orchestrator/DATE/RUN_ID.json` and visible through a viewer script is noted, though the viewer script doesn't exist yet—likely future work
- Two resolved comments clarified a line number discrepancy that's now correct
- This ticket is well-structured overall

**KAN-2** - "Build Junior Developer agent (autonomous easy path)" - Status: Idea
- Blocked by KAN-1
- Has Goal, Decide in this ticket, Deliverables, Open question
- The acceptance criteria has a malformed path: `.github/agent-logs/junior-dev//.json` with an empty segment—should follow the jira-ticket convention of `.github/agent-logs/junior-dev/<DATE>/<RUN_ID>.json`
- The description references the correct convention at line 109 but includes the broken path, which would confuse an implementer
- I need to fix this path issue

**KAN-3** - "Build Testing agent (writes & runs tests, loops back on fail)" - Status: Idea
- Blocked by KAN-2, well-structured with goal, deliverables, loop-back mechanism, and testable acceptance criteria
- The run log path `.github/agent-logs/testing/pr-<n>/<run-id>.json` looks correct, so this ticket is in good shape

**KAN-4** - "Build Senior Developer agent (spec-driven, human-in-the-loop)" - Status: Idea
- Blocked by KAN-1, has the necessary components including spec-driven flow and human-in-the-loop pause
- References `/openspec:explore` as a skill/command that needs verification inside the action, and the ticket acknowledges this uncertainty appropriately
- The human-in-the-loop section presents options to pick and document, which is suitable for a design-in-the-ticket task
- All three acceptance criteria are clear: spec artifact generation with pause, human approval resuming into implementation, and run logs committed to `.github/agent-logs/senior-dev/DATE/RUN_ID.json`

**KAN-5** - "Build PR Refinement agent (counter-voice to PR Review)" - Status: Idea
- No blocking dependencies, has goal, deliverables, model choice, stop condition, and acceptance criteria defined
- The description correctly references the log path pattern from the pr-review workflow, pointing to the DEST variable assignment that establishes the convention for where run logs should be stored
- KAN-5 has no blocking link but could potentially depend on the PR Review agent being completed first

, though since that agent already exists and is functional, KAN-5 can proceed independently
- The description is complete with solid acceptance criteria, so I'll move on

Now I'm noticing KAN-2 has a malformed path in its acceptance criteria: "Run log committed under .github/agent-logs/junior-dev//.json" is missing the date and run ID segments and has double slashes, which would be confusing for implementation. I need to fix this description. The description needs to follow the four-part rubric format when I rewrite it, so I should restructure KAN-2 to clearly separate the goal, context/decision points, deliverables, and acceptance criteria. The key fix is correcting that malformed path in the AC to match the proper format from the jira-ticket convention. The stop condition needs to be exercised in a deliberate failure scenario, and the branch naming convention defined in the junior-dev README should be referenced by the testing workflow as a pull request filter. The core issue is that broken path in the acceptance criteria—it should include the `<DATE>/<RUN_ID>` segments to match the actual jira-ticket convention. I'll rewrite the full description with that path corrected while preserving all the substantive content. I need to nail down three design decisions: whether to use /goal or /ralph-loop framing and justify the choice, pick a stop condition to prevent infinite loops (max-turns, attempt counter, or time budget), and establish a branch naming convention that the Testing agent can rely on. The context is that this junior dev path handles straightforward tickets to skip the overhead of human review, and the Testing agent downstream depends on the branch naming scheme I define here.

For acceptance criteria, I'm checking that dispatching a real ticket produces a branch and PR, the run logs follow the same pattern as the existing jira-ticket workflow, the chosen stop condition actually gets tested in a failure scenario, and the branch convention gets documented in the README. I need to flesh out the remaining deliverables and acceptance criteria. The workflow file should follow the same pattern as the jira-ticket workflow, with the run logs stored in the standardized directory structure. I also need to decide on a stop condition to prevent infinite loops—either a max-turns flag, an attempt counter on the ticket itself, or a time budget on the workflow—and document that choice. The acceptance criteria should verify that dispatching with a real ticket produces a branch and PR, that logs land in the right place, and that the chosen stop mechanism works as intended. PR Review already exists in the repo, so KAN-5 doesn't need a blocking dependency on it. Looking at the workflow, PR Refinement operates on PRs that already have PR Review comments, but it's not part of the core development chain that KAN-1 and KAN-4 establish.

My conclusion is to fix the broken log path in KAN-2's acceptance criteria (changing `.github/agent-logs/junior-dev//.json` to the correct format with date and run ID placeholders), while leaving KAN-1, KAN-3, and KAN-4 as they are since they're well-formed and testable.

input.command:
jira issue edit KAN-2 --no-input -b "Goal
Pick up a ticket flagged \"easy\" by the orchestrator, plan straight through to implementation in a headless Claude Code session, push a branch, open a PR.

Decide in this ticket
Whether the prompt frames the work as /goal or /ralph-loop (both named in docs/agentic-workflow.md). Pick one and document why in the README.

Deliverables
* agents/junior-dev/prompt.md — prompt structure matching agents/jira-ticket/prompt.md.
* agents/junior-dev/README.md.
* .github/workflows/junior-dev.yml — modeled on .github/workflows/jira-ticket.yml. Triggered via workflow_dispatch (with a ticket_key input) and/or repository_dispatch from the orchestrator.

Open question to resolve
Stop condition so the loop cannot run forever on a hard ticket. Options:
- --max-turns N on the action.
- An attempt counter committed back to the ticket as a comment / label.
- Time-budget on the workflow.
Pick one and document the rationale.

Acceptance criteria
* Dispatching the workflow with a real KAN ticket key produces a branch and a PR.
* Run log committed under .github/agent-logs/junior-dev/<DATE>/<RUN_ID>.json (matching the jira-ticket convention at .github/workflows/jira-ticket.yml:97-114).
* The chosen stop condition is exercised in a deliberate failure scenario.
* The branch naming convention is defined in agents/junior-dev/README.md so .github/workflows/testing.yml can reference it as a pull_request filter."

input.content:
*What I did*
• KAN-2: fixed broken log path in Acceptance Criteria

*Why*
The AC contained `.github/agent-logs/junior-dev//.json` — a double-slash with missing `<DATE>/<RUN_ID>` segments, likely a templating artifact. Corrected to `.github/agent-logs/junior-dev/<DATE>/<RUN_ID>.json`, consistent with the jira-ticket convention at `.github/workflows/jira-ticket.yml:97-114`. A broken path in AC means two implementers would ship different log structures and the testing step would fail to find the log file.

*What I skipped*
• KAN-1: In Progress, description accurate (line refs 97-114 verified), two comments already resolved the only prior discrepancy — no action needed.
• KAN-3: Well-formed; Goal, Deliverables, Loop-back mechanism, and AC all present and testable.
• KAN-4: Well-formed; spec-driven flow and human-in-the-loop pause are clearly described with testable AC.
• KAN-5: Well-formed; references `pr-review.yml:57` (verified — that's the `DEST` line with the correct log path pattern).
